tldr: Moving from Akamai to Netlify makes sense when the workload is primarily a frontend site or JAMstack app — Netlify is a hosting and edge platform built around git deploys, not a like-for-like CDN replacement for arbitrary origins.
why_switch: Simple freemium pricing for frontend workloads
Built-in forms, functions, and edge handlers
Faster onboarding for static and SSR sites
Avoid Akamai enterprise contracts for simple frontends
what_to_export: Origin and frontend source code (already in git, ideally)
export_method: Confirm the application source is fully in git — Netlify deploys from a repo, not from a generic origin. Inventory hostnames, redirects, and rewrite rules from Akamai Property Manager. Document edge logic from EdgeWorkers as plain code so you can rewrite it. Export DNS zone records and TLS certificate coverage details. Note any non-frontend workloads (APIs, large media, video) — those should not be moved to Netlify, only the frontend portion. Capture access controls and any signed-URL setup as inventory.
import_method: Connect the git repo to Netlify and configure the build command and publish directory. Add custom domains and let Netlify provision managed TLS, or upload custom certificates if needed. Translate Akamai redirects and rewrites into a _redirects file or netlify.toml. Move edge logic into Netlify Functions (Node.js, AWS Lambda runtime) or Edge Functions (Deno-based) — these are not drop-in replacements for EdgeWorkers and should be rewritten. For non-frontend workloads, keep them on a separate origin and route via DNS rather than forcing them through Netlify. Lower DNS TTLs before the cutover so you can roll back quickly.
effort: 1-4 weeks for frontend-only workloads, longer if Akamai also fronts APIs or media
gotchas: Netlify is built for frontend / JAMstack — it is not a generic CDN for arbitrary origins
Edge Functions run on Deno and have a different runtime than EdgeWorkers; expect a rewrite
Redirect and rewrite syntax is file-based, not GUI-based; review carefully
Bandwidth and function invocations are metered; model cost on real traffic
Some Akamai security features (advanced WAF, bot management) have no direct Netlify equivalent

email.subject: Cancellation and offboarding from our Akamai contract
email.body:
Hi Akamai team,

We are moving our frontend hosting to another platform over the next 14 days and plan to wind down our Akamai usage. Could you confirm the steps to export our property configurations, certificate metadata, log delivery settings, and any account data we are entitled to before the contract ends?

We would like to schedule cancellation at the end of the current contract term. Please share the date by which we need to give written notice to avoid auto-renewal, the offboarding checklist your team uses, and any data retention timelines that apply after the account closes.

If there are minimum commits or remaining contractual obligations we should be aware of, please flag them.

Thanks,
Your customer
